transcript.text: Call.
Happy Friday.
This meeting is being recorded.
Happy Friday.
Okay, so I spoke to Mark just right before this call, and I pulled Yasina in because she's been on this growthx journey with me, and, you know, she's responsible for that department.
So it really just boils down to two concerns that we want to mitigate, which is one being, like, this is an industry that's built on trust.

And if we are sharing or publishing misinformation about rules and regulations and guidelines that are different from, you know, what the standard setting bodies have said, then, like, we look like we don't know what we're talking about.
So our number one concern would be, like, we know that, you know, tone and, you know, all that stuff will be adopted, like, pretty easily, but the accuracy of the call.
Content, because it is sometimes quite technical, and just to ensure, like, how does that happen?
Like, what can we dictate where y'all pull that information from, so it's not just, like, ChatGPT making stuff up?
Yeah, for sure.

I mean, I think the fact-checker agent that we have, that workflow where anything we've drafted, we take every single passage, and we do deep research, answering questions against credible sources for every passage in the article.
That is a good step that we do to ensure accuracy.
The other step that's very important is your team being able to review the content each time before we stage and publish.
I think those are, like, the two biggest things that we do with accuracy.
But if there's, like, a question you want me to run by, Marcel, I'm happy to do it.
I'm just curious, do you have any information on, like, how active
Your judges have been, like, so, like, how many edits have come back or how many, like, you know, I don't know if that's detail that you have.
I don't have anything offhand, to be honest with you.

Like, I understand that, you we would QA it before it gets posted, but it's like, if we're spending a lot of time rewriting all the things that have already been written, then we're not really capitalizing on the advantage of having growthx, like, write it to begin with.

So, like, just curious if it's, you know, 90% of the way there, or is it, like, 60% of the way there, which I think we won't really know until we start, like, trying, which sort of brings me to my next point, which is at what point, I know you mentioned the last time we spoke, in that Phase 1, we will see content produced during Phase 1 so that we know what Phase 2 could look like.
How far into Phase 1 does that happen?
For sure.
Typically, it's happening in the second half of phase one.
So as fast as we can move together to get the artifacts in a place where you feel really confident about the context that feeds into our workflows, the faster we can start publishing.
For some clients, you know, that can happen as soon as week four.
For others, it could be like week five or six.

I think given how motivated you all are to see the output so that you can opt out of phase two if you don't feel confident about the way that we're going to be working, we would be publishing, right, like closer to week four with you guys most likely.
Okay.
Okay.
Yasina, do you have any questions?
So how many, like, pieces of content would we then realistically be able to have a look at before the week seven mark hits?
Yeah, I think, like, ten articles would probably...
They'd a good benchmark for us to work towards.
It can be more, right?
Again, we just need to move fast together so that our team is able to hit whatever bar you want us to reach.

But I think if you told the team, like, hey, our decision to move to Phase 1 is really based on seeing examples of publish-ready content, then our team would, like, make that, right, their top priority when it comes to success in Phase 1.
It's tricky because there's no, like, number that we're committing to in Phase 1.
We commit to five per week in Phase 2.
But we do understand that, like, you know, seeing what we're going to be able to scale out and continue to improve upon, you know, it's really important, right?
So I think 10 is probably a good specific number to give you.
And if that's not enough, then just tell our team up front that, you know, for
For you to make a confident decision on Phase 2, you need to see at least 15 or at least 20, right?
Whatever that number is.
Okay.
And then asking one more time if there is a shorter time period that we could commit to for Phase 2 that would look more like six months as opposed to 12.
Yeah, I hear you and I wish I could be flexible here.
The reality is that I'll have like over 10 clients that want every single date that we have through mid-November.
And it makes, you know, my life crazy because like how do we choose the right clients, you know?
And like when we tell a client that the kickoff date is going to be at a certain time, like we don't want to change that.
And so I've had to change, you know, like the way that I communicate and I think, you know, field guides and shouldn't move forward with.
Growthx, unless you're really feeling confident that this is the strategy that you want to take when it comes to growing organically, right?
If you're feeling like, you know, hey, like we want to leverage paid channels like that, that is going to have a better ROI for us.
If you're feeling like you don't want to make an investment for 12 months to create, you know, high quality content at scale connected to what your audience is focused on.
Then I would say, you know, like, maybe wait until next year and then, right, come, come back to us and we'll have a lot more capacity created.
We'll have like a lower entry point created as well.
But if you want to get in with us at a time where like every single client we bring on, churn is like the most important metric to our business.
It's not, it's not revenue growth right now.
We're going to do everything in our power, right, to make you successful.
And, you know, I think like town.com, it's a great example of, you know, they trusted us to build out there.
everything our in power.
And this is tax information.
So it's similar to the accuracy that you all need.
And you could look at some of those reference articles to see how you feel about the quality of some of the content that we've written.
But yeah, it would be the 12 months.
It would be the terms that we put out there.
I think you and I talked, Jenny, about doing quarterly on the billing last time, right?
Right.
So we can do preferred and great.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So we can definitely do that.

But I think, you know, unless there's like specific questions that you guys can like email us and I can work with the team to get you a quick answer around, like we would just have to give up October 20th if you guys weren't ready to make a decision sometime like early next week.
Yeah.
think these are kind of the last two questions that I, that I had, and then Yassine and I can talk and then we just get back to Mark with, with our, you know, go, no, go plan.
We did hear back from finance today.
Stay there.
Supportive of our budget ask increase for this year, which does give us the ability to move quickly.
It's just now Yasine and Mark and I aligning.
So we have finance approval.
We've got, you know, that hurdle has been overcome, which is great.
So we just have to make sure this is like the right long-term strategy.
Yeah.
Yeah, and it makes perfect sense.
And I apologize, like, that I have to, you know, communicate this way.
It's just, it's just, I'm between a rock and a hard place.
And I'm not sure what the answer is.
Yeah.
Transparency is just, is helpful.
So like, don't feel bad.
Okay.
Well, in the remaining 10 or so minutes that I had scheduled for this meeting, let Yasina and I chat.
And then she and I can send Mark a Slack with our recommendation so that we can at least get back to next week with like, we are ready to move forward.
Or like, we have to put a pin in it.
And please release our date.
So we can answer one of those ways.
That's perfect.
And I think, you know, if you keep Marcel copied on any email that you send with questions, it's a lot easier for me to, you know, have his visibility around that and get quicker answers.
But I know it's not an easy decision.
I will say that, you know, if you're trying to create content to show up in AI answers at scale, there's no better partner than growthx, that's for sure.
But if there's other strategies that are top of mind, it could make sense the way.
Yeah.
No, I appreciate all the information.
And Yasine and I are going to chat right now.
Yasine, I'll just send you a Slack you link right now.
We can chat.
Okay, cool.
Tyler, thank you so much.
Enjoy the rest of your Friday and have a good weekend.
Thanks,'all.
You too.
See you later.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss concerns and details about implementing growthx.ai's content creation services for Fieldguide.](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Fieldguide's main concerns: content accuracy in a trust-based industry and efficiency of the content creation process](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=5.0)
  - [growthx.ai uses fact-checker agents and client review to ensure accuracy; 10+ publish-ready articles expected by week 4-6 of Phase 1](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=76.0)
  - [growthx.ai maintains a strict 12-month commitment for Phase 2, with quarterly billing option available](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=434.0)
  - [Fieldguide received finance approval for budget increase, decision pending internal alignment](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=478.0)

## Topics

### Content Accuracy Concerns

  - [Industry built on trust; misinformation risks damaging credibility](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=33.0)
  - [growthx.ai's approach:](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=18.0)
      - [Fact-checker agent workflow: deep research against credible sources for every passage](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=76.0)
      - [Client team review before staging and publishing](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=95.0)
  - [No specific data on judge activity or edit frequency available](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)

### Content Creation Efficiency

  - [Concern: extensive rewrites could negate time-saving benefits](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=133.0)
  - [Unclear current efficiency (60% vs. 90% ready)](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=149.0)
  - [Real assessment possible only after trial period](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=149.0)

### Phase 1 Content Production Timeline

  - [Content production typically starts in second half of Phase 1](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
  - [For motivated clients like Fieldguide: as early as week 4](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=201.0)
  - [Target: \~10 publish-ready articles before week 7](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
  - [Flexible based on client needs (e.g., 15-20 articles if required for confident decision-making)](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)

### Phase 2 Commitment and Terms

  - [growthx.ai maintains strict 12-month commitment for Phase 2](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=434.0)
  - [High demand for available slots (10+ clients for mid-November dates)](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=334.0)
  - [Quarterly billing option confirmed](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=438.0)
  - [Tyler emphasized importance of client confidence in organic growth strategy](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)

### Fieldguide's Internal Decision-Making

  - [Finance approval received for budget increase](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=478.0)
  - [Final decision pending alignment between Jenny, Yasine, and Mark](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=468.0)
  - [Commitment to provide go/no-go decision to growthx.ai early next week](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0)

### growthx.ai's Positioning

  - [Presented as best partner for creating content to appear in AI answers at scale](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=562.0)
  - [Referenced town.com as example of successful implementation with similar accuracy requirements](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=425.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Jenny and Yasine to discuss internally immediately after the call](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0)
  - [Send recommendation to Mark via Slack](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0)
  - [Provide go/no-go decision to growthx.ai early next week](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0)
  - [If proceeding, prepare for potential October 20th start date](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [If needed, email additional questions to growthx.ai team (copy Marcel for quicker responses)](https://fathom.video/share/fZuVizoEGr1dzxNVBjpCXcLxdwDSoagx?tab=summary&timestamp=547.0)

action_items.description: If needed, email specific Qs to Tyler (cc Marcel) for quick answers re growthx.ai partnership
Inform Tyler early next week re decision to proceed or not w/ growthx.ai Oct 20 start date
Chat w/ Yasine re growthx.ai decision; send recommendation to Mark via Slack
